description: data/make_data.py's customers dimension table (data/customers.csv) has a duplicate row for customer_id 'cust_0'. transform() left-joins orders onto this dimension table (via pipeline.extract.extract_customers) to enrich with region before aggregating. Because the join key isn't unique in the dimension table, every order row for cust_0 is duplicated (fanned out) by the merge, silently doubling its aggregated total_revenue and order_count.
expected_failure: tests/test_pipeline.py::test_known_aggregate fails: pinned cust_0 Dec-2025 aggregate (3, 423.10) becomes (6, 846.20) — exactly doubled by the one duplicated dimension row
fix_hint: Deduplicate data/customers.csv on customer_id (or assert/validate uniqueness before the merge in transform.py) so the region join is one-to-one and doesn't fan out matching order rows.
